source_pr.title: planner: fix do-statement will error with agg function
source_pr.body:
Signed-off-by: ailinkid <314806019@qq.com>

<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/31413

### What is changed and how it works?

Reason: In normal build-phase, the aggFunc is built ahead of other select fields, informing the latter one to skip rewrite progress of the agg field via aggMapper. While we miss this when handling do-statement.

Like comments down on the issue, do-statement can only contain expressions, in which the aggFunc may take place, leading the name-resolve problem.

Approach: build the aggMapper first like what it does in the building-select

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

### Release note

<!-- bugfix or new feature needs a release note -->

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: - // Use the testSerialSuite to fix the unstable test | tk.MustExec("DO GROUP_CONCAT(NULLIF(ELT(1, @e), 2.0) ORDER BY 1)") (executor/simple_test.go)
failure_signature: - // Use the testSerialSuite to fix the unstable test | tk.MustExec("DO GROUP_CONCAT(NULLIF(ELT(1, @e), 2.0) ORDER BY 1)") (executor/simple_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: - // Use the testSerialSuite to fix the unstable test | tk.MustExec("DO GROUP_CONCAT(NULLIF(ELT(1, @e), 2.0) ORDER BY 1)") (executor/simple_test.go)
